channel.title: Publications on Hanlin Ma
channel.description: Recent content in Publications on Hanlin Ma

item.title: An Ethical Analysis of Moral Enhancement: A Free-Will Perspective
item.description: This article develops a free-will-centered analysis of moral enhancement and considers the ethical implications of interventionist approaches to moral agency.

item.title: Bringing Web 3.0 and DAO into Democratic Class: A Study of Pedagogy in Higher Education
item.description: This paper reflects an interest in technology, governance, and higher-education pedagogy.

item.title: Building an Interdisciplinary Knowledge Cultivation System: The Case of PPE Education
item.description: This article reflects on PPE education as a model for interdisciplinary knowledge formation and curriculum design in higher education.

item.title: Cyberpunk and Cypherpunk: A Philosophical Analysis Comparing Two Views of the Metaverse
item.description: This conference paper compares cyberpunk and cypherpunk perspectives as rival philosophical frameworks for interpreting the metaverse.

item.title: Life-Care Education Based on Improved Mental Models
item.description: Edited volume on life-care education and improved mental models.

item.title: Limits of AI Moral Enhancement
item.description: This article addresses the conceptual and ethical limits of AI-driven moral enhancement and situates the debate within broader concerns about autonomy and technological intervention.

item.title: Meaning of Life and One Health
item.description: Edited volume on life, meaning, and the One Health framework.

item.title: On Isaiah Berlin's "Two Concepts of Liberty"
item.description: An early article engaging a classic question in political philosophy through Isaiah Berlin’s distinction between negative and positive liberty.

item.title: R.U.R. (Rossum's Universal Robots)
item.description: Chinese translation of Karel Capek’s classic work on robots.

item.title: Research on Self-Deception: Intentionalism vs. Non-intentionalism
item.description: This article compares competing philosophical accounts of self-deception and explores how debates on intentionalism bear on agency, rationality, and moral psychology.

item.title: The Metaphysical Foundations of Rawls's Moral Constructivism
item.description: This article examines the metaphysical assumptions that underwrite Rawlsian moral constructivism and situates the argument within contemporary normative theory.

item.title: The Second-Person Standpoint and Moral Machine
item.description: This conference paper is part of a broader research agenda on second-person perspectives, ethics, and intelligent systems.

item.title: The Significance of a Second-Person Perspective for the Development of Humanoid AI
item.description: This paper argues for the importance of second-person perspectives in the conceptual development of humanoid AI.

item.title: The War of Autonomous Driving: Computer Vision and LiDAR
item.description:
This English version is translated from the original Chinese publication.

English Translation

In recent years, a “war of autonomous driving” has been unfolding in the field of intelligent vehicles. On April 22, 2019, Elon Reeve Musk declared at Tesla’s Autonomy Day event that using LiDAR was futile and that anyone relying on it was bound to fail. At the time, aside from Tesla’s commitment to computer vision, most autonomous-driving developers treated LiDAR as the core sensor for self-driving vehicles and as a key device for building machine perception. Musk’s remarks therefore caused a major response in the industry and helped bring the conflict between computer vision and LiDAR to the center of debate.

item.title: World History Adventure: Pirate Legends
item.description: Translated volume in the World History Adventure series.

item.title: World History Adventure: The Rise of the United States
item.description: Translated volume in the World History Adventure series.

item.title: World History Adventure: Town Life
item.description: Translated volume in the World History Adventure series.
